name: dilated_h64_0_90
description: The 0.9001 contest-CUDA baseline (2026-04-25). Dilated h=64 renderer + matched poses + AV1 CRF=50 masks.

stats.note: Distribution is LARGE-VALUED (max 35, std 11). These are NOT raw frame-pair PoseNet outputs (which would be ~0±1). They look like cumulative trajectory or specially-scaled FiLM conditioning values. Critical: any pose TTO that re-initializes from extract_gt_pose_targets() instead of these poses will land in the wrong distribution and produce 23x worse PoseNet (LANE-B 2026-04-26 confirmed this).

masks.mkv.note: NOT SAVED HERE (411KB, regenerable from upstream SegNet on GT video at CRF=50). To rebuild: see scripts/build_baseline_masks.sh.

provenance.reason: User invoked durability-guarantee on baseline poses after LANE-B (2026-04-26) wasted 6.5h initializing TTO from the wrong pose distribution. Original location was untracked (experiments/results/ is gitignored).

lane_b_post_mortem.what_lane_b_did_wrong: Initialized init_poses = extract_gt_pose_targets(gt_frames, posenet) — frame-pair PoseNet outputs, distribution ~0±1. The renderer expects FiLM conditioning in the LARGE distribution shown above (mean 5.2, max 35). The 1000-step Adam couldn't recover from the orders-of-magnitude wrong init.
lane_b_post_mortem.what_lane_b_should_have_done: Pass --gt-poses-path submissions/baseline_dilated_h64_0_90/optimized_poses.bin to the bootstrap. optimize_poses.py line 690-692 honors this and uses the canonical poses as warm start.
